'''Dean Richard Widders''' (born 25 October 1979) is an Anaiwan [[Indigenous Australians|Indigenous Australian]] former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. He works with the [[National Rugby League]] as an Indigenous Pathways manager.  He is coach of the [[Parramatta Eels Women]] team in the [[2022 NRL Women's season|2022 NRLW season]].

==Early life==
Widders was born in [[Armidale, NSW|Armidale]] in New South Wales, Australia.{{Citation needed|date=June 2021}}

He played his first game of [[rugby league]] at the age of six for the local Armidale colts after watching his father Jake Widders who was a player for the famous Aboriginal team the Narwan Eels.{{Citation needed|date=June 2021}}

During Widders' teenage years he honed his rugby league playing for the Armidale Greens and Narwan Eels.{{Citation needed|date=June 2021}} During this time, Arthur Beetson [[Sydney Roosters]] selector flew to Armidale to see Widders play.

==Playing career==
===NRL===
In 1996, at the age of 17, Widders moved to Sydney to play with the [[Sydney Roosters]].{{Citation needed|date=June 2021}} In this year, he also captained an [[Indigenous Australians|Aboriginal]] side.{{Citation needed|date=June 2021}}

Widders made his debut for the Roosters in 2000, playing in 13 NRL matches for the club.{{Citation needed|date=June 2021}} In 2002, Widders signed with the [[Parramatta Eels]]; in 2005 he was part of The Parramatta side that won the minor premiership. He finished with the club at the end of 2006.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/news/league/minor-premiers-mourn-loss-of-hindmarsh/2005/09/02/1125302743907.html|title = Minor premiers mourn loss of Hindmarsh|date = 2 September 2005}}</ref>

Widders was involved in a [[racial vilification]] incident in July 2005 when he was abused by [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] captain [[Bryan Fletcher (rugby league)|Bryan Fletcher]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/news/league/fletcher-in-race-row/2005/07/03/1120329328346.html|title = Fletcher in race row|date = 4 July 2005}}</ref> In 2006, he signed a three-year contract to play for the South Sydney Rabbitohs starting in 2007.{{Citation needed|date=June 2021}}

===Castleford Tigers===
Widders joined Castleford for the 2009 season. He played 19 times in his first season for the Tigers, scoring 6 tries.{{Citation needed|date=June 2021}}

Widders picked up an injury and missed 2 months of the season but came back and played in an unfamiliar role of {{Rlp|SO}}, linking up with [[Rangi Chase]].{{citation needed|date=May 2017}}

Widders played in 2011, his final season at the Castleford Tigers. The club failed to make the playoffs after a narrow defeat by Hull KR. Widders played his final game with Castleford in the last home game against [[Hull F.C.]]

===Off-field===
In 1997, Widders returned to Duval High School in Armidale to complete his Higher School Certificate.

In 2004, Widders was awarded the NRL's [[Ken Stephen Medal]] for his positive work with youth in the community and, in 2006, he was appointed to the Federal Government's National Indigenous Council. Widders is also an Ambassador for the National Aboriginal Sports Corporation Australia (NASCA).<ref>{{cite news|publisher=Commonwealth Government|title=New Member Appointed to National Indigenous Council|url=http://www.vibe.com.au/dsounds/archive/read.asp?id=698|accessdate=2008-01-02|date=2006-06-19|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070920093708/http://www.vibe.com.au/dsounds/archive/read.asp?id=698|archivedate=20 September 2007|url-status=dead }}</ref>

In November 2018, Widders participated in the Legends of League tournament for Parramatta, which was held at the [[Central Coast Stadium]] in Gosford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2018/11/19/parramatta-at-legends-of-league-tournament/|title=Parramatta at Legends of League tournament|date=18 November 2018}}</ref>

He is coach of the [[Parramatta Eels Women]] team in the [[2022 NRL Women's season|2022 NRLW season]].

==Career highlights==
*''Junior Club:'' Armidale, Narwan
*''Career Stats:'' 219 first grade games scoring 59 tries
*''Representative Honours:'' Country Origin (2006), Prime Minister's XIII (2007)
*''Honours:'' Minor Premiership (2005) with Parramatta
